Figure 1. Overview of RUMS. RUMS-Utility quantifies how much memory subsets reduce response uncertainty (training phase), while RUMS-Models efficiently selects informative items at inference.

Table 2. Average win rate of our method against baselines on Synthetic (left) and Real World (right) datasets. Cells in green mark cases where our method outperforms the baseline, and cells in red mark cases where the baseline does better. Values with magnitude above 0.1 are bolded to highlight strong differences. Results are reported for two response models (LLaMA 70B Instruct and GPT-4) and two RUMS variants: RUMS-Binary (fewer items) and RUMS-Multi (more items).

Figure 24. Optimal threshold calculated for each n = 10 users based on utility scores. It shows the optimal thresholds for the 10 users (solid blue line), as well as the mean threshold (dotted red line) and ±1 standard deviation (gray shaded area)
